Well that took some minutes away from somebody else in the skincare business. So she got somebody else's territory. Joan sells food. That took some minutes away from somebody else. So she is able to leverage the getting and keeping of one kind of territory into getting and keeping another kind of territory and getting and keeping another kind of territory. When you think about that, you think about it in the context of your business and whatever space that you work in, right? So your customers, whoever it is you sell to now, who give you money for whatever they give you money for, are giving money to somebody else for something else. Now some of those something else's, there's no way, shape or form, opportunity that would occur to you to make them relevant and step into that territory. So John Keele's customers who buy how to make money on the internet from him are also buying ground beef to eat. John probably will not be able to figure out some sort of logical path to get him into the ground beef business. Trump figured out a path to get into stakes by the way, but John probably won't. But there are things John's customers are buying and spending money on that he could make a logical path to. And either proprietary create things for them or as John go find somebody that he can connect leveraging these multiple assets. And the other thing you should remember about that, and it's particular, how many of you in here are info marketers, fuller part, it's part of your business? That's a significant part of the room. But it applies to others as well. But I'll give it to you in the info marketer analogy because you'll understand it easiest. But a lot of people miss its importance. When somebody buys you as their guru of something. So on a non-info marketer level, let's say you're Kurt, so you're their money manager, investment manager, and financial guy. On an info marketer level, it's easier to understand it. So the entry door for Dan is almost entirely marketing. They come because of they want marketing and they buy them as a marketing advisor. When someone buys you as their guru of something, their fitness, their finances, their chiropractic care, their time management guy, their marketing guy, their whatever. They are eager, irrationally eager for you to be their guru of all sorts of other things too. Now it makes no sense. So my joke about this has always been, in all my mastermind meetings, and anybody that runs mastermind meetings of a business kind, you know this without me telling you this, if you keep people around, if they stay in the guru. After about somewhere between the third meeting and the sixth meeting, the conversations begin to morph from business stuff to personal and emotional stuff. Because there's more of that. And the business stuff, once people start doing well, and they've got eight or ten good tools in their toolbox, there's less and less reason really to sit around and talk about, gee, will you critique my latest sales letter? And now people want to talk about their childhood issues and their parents and their near-do-well relatives and their kids and their spouse and their employees. So it's that stuff. So I, in all of these settings, and I always find it funny, but it was really funny when I got divorced. In all of these settings, they have this conversation where they are asking me for relationship advice.
